checkedCadence: Weekly source check; urgent changes can be added through the Signal Intel Desk daily task.
method: Sourced, dated figures only. No fake live counters. Figures are category-specific and not interchangeable. When no reliable global total exists, the panel states that clearly rather than inventing one.

panels.title: Global Conflict Snapshot
panels.figure: NO SINGLE GLOBAL WAR DEATH COUNTER
panels.description: Conflict deaths and civilian casualties must be tracked by theater because Gaza, Ukraine, Sudan, Myanmar, Yemen, Congo, Syria, and other wars use different reporting systems, verification standards, and political definitions.
panels.sourceLabel: UN/OHCHR/OCHA / ICRC / Reuters where primary unavailable

panels.title: Global Displacement
panels.description: UNHCR's 2026 Global Trends reporting put forced displacement at about 117.8 million people in 2025, including refugees, asylum seekers, internally displaced people, and others needing protection. Keep refugees, IDPs, asylum seekers, and stateless people separate.

panels.title: Famine / Acute Food Insecurity
panels.description: FAO and WFP warned in June 2026 that acute hunger may worsen in 13 hotspots from June to November 2026. IPC phases, famine warnings, siege conditions, and humanitarian access restrictions must be tracked separately.

panels.title: Migration / Irregular Immigration
panels.description: Border encounters, irregular crossings, asylum applications, removals, returns, departures, missing migrants, overstays, and estimated unauthorized populations are separate categories. Do not combine them into one illegal-immigration counter.

panels.title: Migration Deaths / Missing Migrants
panels.description: Mediterranean and desert-route deaths are undercounted. AP reported IOM figures of more than 800 dead or missing on the Central Mediterranean route from January to mid-May 2026, plus continuing shipwreck incidents.

panels.title: Vaccines: Damages & Compensation
panels.status: Compensation lane, not raw adverse-event lane
panels.description: Adverse-event reports, compensated claims, pending claims, rejected claims, and causal findings must be separated. VAERS reports are signals, not proof of causation. Use current HRSA monthly reports for live totals.
panels.sourceLabel: HRSA VICP / CICP / UK VDPS / VAERS disclaimer

panels.title: Children / Trafficking / Missing
panels.description: NCMEC reported 21.3 million CyberTipline reports in 2025, 32,167 missing-child cases supported, 29,013 resolved cases, and 1.4 million online enticement reports. Missing-child cases, online exploitation reports, trafficking indicators, and resolved cases are different categories and must not be merged.

panels.title: Missing Children Cases Supported
panels.description: NCMEC-supported missing-child cases are not the same as total missing children worldwide, trafficking victims, or CyberTipline reports. They are a U.S.-focused support-case category, including some young adults ages 18-20.

panels.title: Online Enticement Reports
panels.description: Online enticement reports are suspected online exploitation reports. They are not a missing-child count and not proof of trafficking without investigation.

panels.title: Medical / System Failure
panels.description: Hospital attacks, medicine shortages, blocked aid, disease outbreaks, healthcare worker deaths, excess mortality, and medical-system collapse should be tracked by conflict zone or country, not merged into a global claim.
panels.sourceLabel: WHO / OCHA / national health authorities / court records
